title: What to know about Rhodiola rosea before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Rhodiola rosea are the relief of anxiety through its ability to reduce cortisol levels and increase the neurotransmitter serotonin, which is also responsible for the alleviation of depression. Additionally, Rhodiola rosea is used to combat fatigue by increasing the production of ATP and enhancing mitochondrial function, while also mitigating stress through its adaptogenic properties that help the body adapt to stress. The plant's constituents, including rosmarinic acid and rosavin, have been shown to improve sleep quality and duration in individuals with insomnia. Furthermore, Rhodiola rosea has been used to treat burnout and prevent memory loss by protecting against oxidative stress and promoting neuroprotection. Rhodiola rosea has also been used to alleviate symptoms of ADHD and sleep disorders, such as sleep apnea and narcolepsy, and has been shown to lower blood pressure in individuals with hypertension.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Rhodiola rosea are extensive, with its antioxidant properties attributed to the reduction of oxidative stress, while its anti-inflammatory effects are mediated by the inhibition of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. The bioactive compounds responsible for these properties include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. Rhodiola rosea has been found to exhibit antistress and adaptogenic properties, enhancing the body's ability to cope with stress and promoting balance in the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, which is regulated by neuroactive compounds such as rosavin and salidroside. Additionally, the plant has neuroprotective effects, preventing neuronal damage and apoptosis, and exhibits antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of certain microorganisms. Rhodiola rosea also possesses cytotoxic, antiproliferative, and immunomodulatory properties, demonstrating its potential as a therapeutic agent in the treatment of various diseases, including cancer and autoimmune disorders.
identification: Rhodiola rosea is a perennial flowering plant of the family Crassulaceae, native to high-altitude, arctic, and alpine regions in Europe, Asia, and the Arctic tundra. It grows to 10-30 centimeters in height, with a thick, fleshy, and ovate rootstock and a simple, glabrous stem bearing alternate, ovate, and crenate leaves that are 2-6 centimeters long and 1-3 centimeters wide. The plant produces white or pinkish flowers in a dense, terminal raceme, with four petals and four sepals that are lanceolate or spatulate in shape. Rhodiola rosea is commonly found in areas with rocky terrain, alpine meadows, and mountain streams, typically at elevations of 1,500-3,000 meters above sea level. Its geographical range spans across the Arctic tundra and mountainous regions of Scandinavia, Russia, China, Mongolia, and parts of Europe and Asia.
cultivation: Rhodiola rosea is a perennial plant that thrives in alpine and subalpine regions with well-drained soil, full sun to partial shade, and cold temperatures. It requires a growth period of 120 to 150 frost-free days, typically at elevations above 2,400 meters. To cultivate Rhodiola rosea, it's essential to plant seeds in early spring or fall, and provide adequate moisture, especially during the germination phase. Growth tips for this plant include using raised beds or containers to maintain consistent soil moisture, and avoiding over-fertilization, which can lead to reduced root development and decreased yields. Regular pruning and division can also help promote healthy growth and prevent Rhodiola rosea from becoming too woody.
history: Rhodiola rosea is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in Tibetan and Mongolian cultures, to enhance physical and mental performance, improve mood, and alleviate stress and fatigue. The plant's adaptogenic properties were first documented in the 19th century by Russian scientists, who discovered its ability to stimulate the body's natural defenses and promote resilience to stress. In the 1960s and 1970s, Soviet scientists conducted extensive research on Rhodiola rosea, finding it to be effective in improving mental performance, memory, and mood in individuals under physical or mental stress. The plant's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties have also been studied, with evidence suggesting its potential in treating anxiety, depression, and other mood disorders. Clinical trials have consistently shown Rhodiola rosea to be safe and effective when used in moderation, with most studies recommending a daily dose of 100-200 mg of the plant's extract.

constituents.name: Salidroside
constituents.description: Salidroside is a phenylethanol glycoside, a phenolic glycoside with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Rosavin
constituents.description: Rosavin is a glycoside with adaptogenic properties, improving physical and mental performance, and possessing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.

constituents.name: Tyrosol
constituents.description: Tyrosol is a phenolic compound with antioxidant properties and the ability to scavenge free radicals.

constituents.name: Caffeic acid
constituents.description: Caffeic acid is a phenolic acid found in Rhodiola rosea with antioxidant and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Quercetin
constituents.description: A flavonoid constituent of Rhodiola rosea with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and free radical scavenging properties.

constituents.name: Kaempferol
constituents.description: Kaempferol is a flavonoid glycoside constituent of Rhodiola rosea, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardioprotective properties.

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: Saponins are a group of glycosides found in Rhodiola rosea, characterized by their foaming properties, bitterness, and ability to lower surface tension.

constituents.name: Rosmarinic acid
constituents.description: A polyphenolic compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties.

constituents.name: Glycosides
constituents.description: Glycosides found in Rhodiola rosea are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant and adaptogenic properties.

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.description: Flavonoids are a type of antioxidant found in Rhodiola rosea, possessing anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and free radical scavenging properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids, found in Rhodiola rosea, are a type of antioxidant and have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and free radical-scavenging properties.

constituents.name: Isorhapontigenin
constituents.description: Isorhapontigenin is a phenanthrene derivative with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Rosin
constituents.description: Rosin is a type of resin found in the plant Rhodiola rosea, with properties including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal.

constituents.name: Triterpenoids
constituents.description: Triterpenoids, a constituent of Rhodiola rosea, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Phenylethylamine
constituents.description: A naturally occurring amphetamine alkaloid found in Rhodiola rosea, known for its stimulant, sympathomimetic and vasoconstrictive properties.

constituents.name: Anthocyanins
constituents.description: Anthocyanins are powerful antioxidants with anti-inflammatory properties, free radical scavenging activity, and the ability to inhibit lipid peroxidation.

constituents.name: Phenylethanoid glycosides
constituents.description: Phenylethanoid glycosides are iridoid glycosides with adaptogenic properties, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, and the ability to modulate neurotransmitters and hormones.

constituents.name: Triterpene acids
constituents.description: Triterpene acids in Rhodiola rosea are known for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties.

constituents.name: Rhaponticin
constituents.description: Rhaponticin is a phenylethanoid glycoside found in Rhodiola rosea, possessing anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties.

constituents.name: Tryptophan
constituents.description: Tryptophan is a non-essential amino acid found in Rhodiola rosea with properties including acting as a precursor to serotonin and melatonin, and contributing to the synthesis of proteins and neurotransmitters.

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Rhodiola rosea may cause difficulty falling or staying asleep.
Rhodiola rosea-induced anxiety is reported in individuals with pre-existing anxiety disorders.
Rhodiola rosea causes tension headache and migraine-like headache symptoms in some individuals.
Dizziness is associated with orthostatic hypotension caused by Rhodiola rosea root extracts due to vasomotor effects.
Nausea occurs in some individuals taking Rhodiola rosea.
Increased sensitivity to stress and stimuli
Gastrointestinal irritation and stomach upset have been reported in individuals consuming Rhodiola rosea
Increased sympathetic nervous system activity has been observed in some individuals taking Rhodiola rosea, leading to feelings of restlessness.
Tremors have been reported in some individuals taking Rhodiola rosea supplements.
Rhodiola rosea consumption can lead to physical dependence and withdrawal symptoms upon abrupt cessation.

uses_list.description: This adaptogenic herb has been traditionally used to help alleviate fatigue and exhaustion, possibly due to its ability to increase ATP production, reduce oxidative stress, and improve mitochondrial function, thereby enhancing energy metabolism and reducing feelings of tiredness.

It has been used to help mitigate the effects of stress, possibly by reducing cortisol levels, improving mood, and enhancing resilience, thus providing a natural means to cope with stress and anxiety.

Research suggests that it may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, possibly by reducing cortisol levels, improving mood, and enhancing relaxation response, thus providing a natural means to manage anxiety and promote relaxation.

It has been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of depression, possibly by reducing cortisol levels, improving mood, and enhancing neurotransmitter balance, thus providing a natural means to manage depression and promote well-being.

This adaptogenic herb has been used to help alleviate symptoms of burnout, possibly by reducing stress, improving mood, and enhancing resilience, thus providing a natural means to cope with burnout and promote well-being.

It has been used to help alleviate symptoms of insomnia, possibly by reducing cortisol levels, improving mood, and promoting relaxation, thus providing a natural means to manage insomnia and improve sleep quality.

Research suggests that it may help alleviate symptoms of migraines, possibly by reducing stress, improving mood, and enhancing neurotransmitter balance, thus providing a natural means to manage migraines and reduce frequency and severity.

This adaptogenic herb has been used to help alleviate symptoms of ADHD, possibly by improving attention, reducing stress, and enhancing cognitive function, thus providing a natural means to manage ADHD and improve focus and concentration.

It has been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of memory loss, possibly by improving cognitive function, reducing oxidative stress, and enhancing neuroprotection, thus providing a natural means to manage memory loss and promote cognitive health.

Research suggests that it may help alleviate symptoms of hypertension, possibly by reducing cortisol levels, improving mood, and enhancing cardiovascular health, thus providing a natural means to manage hypertension and reduce blood pressure.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Rhodiola rosea is used to treat anxiety by reducing symptoms of excessive worry and fear. It also helps alleviate stress, which can manifest as fatigue, burnout, insomnia, and sleep disorders. Additionally, Rhodiola rosea has been shown to have antidepressant properties, particularly in mild to moderate depression, by influencing neurotransmitter levels. Furthermore, it has been found to improve exercise performance, reduce the frequency of migraines, and alleviate symptoms of ADHD, such as attention deficits and hyperactivity. Its adaptogenic properties also help manage conditions like hypoglycemia, adrenal fatigue, sleep disturbances, hypertension, mood swings, memory loss, cognitive decline, cognitive impairment, and fibromyalgia.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional Chinese medicine, Rhodiola rosea is used to alleviate anxiety by regulating the body's stress response, thus helping to manage stress and related symptoms such as depression, fatigue, and burnout, while also improving memory loss and insomnia by enhancing sleep quality and promoting relaxation. The adaptogenic properties of Rhodiola rosea also help to alleviate ADHD and sleep disorders by regulating cortisol levels and improving mood swings. Furthermore, it is used to help manage hypertension by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, and hypoglycemia by stabilizing blood sugar levels. Additionally, Rhodiola rosea is used to address adrenal fatigue, chronic fatigue syndrome, and sleep disorders by promoting energy production and improving cognitive function, which includes enhancing memory and reducing migraines. The herb also has a regulatory effect on the adrenal and hypothyroidism systems, promoting balance and well-being.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Rhodiola rosea is used to treat various health conditions. The plant is widely used to alleviate stress and anxiety, which can lead to depression, a condition it has been shown to reduce symptoms of. Rhodiola rosea is also effective in addressing fatigue, burnout, and migraines, and has been found to improve sleep quality, thereby reducing insomnia, while also helping to manage hypertension. Its adaptogenic properties also make it beneficial in treating adrenal fatigue, sleep disturbances, ADHD, mood swings, cognitive decline, sleep disorder, chronic fatigue syndrome, and physical trauma. Additionally, Rhodiola rosea has been found to be useful in treating hypothyroidism, headaches, memory loss, and menopause symptoms.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Rhodiola rosea is used to treat anxiety, stress, and fatigue, with its adaptogenic properties helping to regulate the body's response to these conditions. It is also used to address depression, burnout, and insomnia, promoting a sense of calm and well-being in individuals. Additionally, Rhodiola rosea is employed to alleviate sleep disorders, migraines, hypertension, adrenal fatigue, ADHD, hypoglycemia, chronic fatigue syndrome, and memory loss, showcasing its broad range of therapeutic benefits. Furthermore, it is used to alleviate menopause symptoms, cognitive decline, and cognitive function impairment, as well as mental fatigue, hypothyroidism, and headaches, highlighting its potential as a natural remedy. By addressing these various health conditions, Rhodiola rosea demonstrates its value as a versatile and effective treatment option in unani medicine.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Rhodiola rosea is used to treat stress, anxiety, depression, and fatigue by alleviating symptoms of burnout, insomnia, ADHD, and memory loss. It has been shown to reduce migraine frequency and severity, as well as alleviate symptoms of adrenal fatigue, mood swings, hypoglycemia, and hypertension. Additionally, Rhodiola rosea is used to treat sleep disorders, chronic fatigue syndrome, and hypothyroidism, while also reducing irritability and impotence. Furthermore, it has been found to improve cognitive decline and alleviate symptoms of adrenal insufficiency, making it a potential treatment for individuals experiencing cognitive decline. Its adaptogenic properties also make it a useful treatment for managing stress, which can be a contributing factor to many of these conditions, including migraines, insomnia, and chronic fatigue syndrome.

benefits_list.description: This benefit is due to the presence of salidroside and rosin, which have been shown to have adaptogenic properties, allowing the body to adapt to and resist stress, anxiety, and fatigue by modulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis and regulating cortisol levels.

This benefit is attributed to the presence of phenolic compounds, including salidroside and rosin, which have been found to have a positive effect on neurotransmitter activity, particularly serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine, thereby improving mood and reducing symptoms of depression.
This benefit is due to the presence of salidroside and rosin, which have been shown to increase mitochondrial biogenesis and ATP production, thereby increasing energy levels and reducing fatigue.

This benefit is attributed to the presence of phenolic compounds, including salidroside and rosin, which have been found to have a positive effect on neurotransmitter activity, particularly dopamine and norepinephrine, thereby improving cognitive function, including attention, memory, and processing speed.

This benefit is due to the presence of salidroside and rosin, which have been shown to have anxiolytic properties, reducing anxiety and stress by modulating the HPA axis and regulating cortisol levels.

This benefit is attributed to the presence of phenolic compounds, including salidroside and rosin, which have been found to have a positive effect on metabolism and fat metabolism, thereby supporting weight loss and maintenance.
This benefit is due to the presence of salidroside and rosin, which have been shown to increase mitochondrial biogenesis and ATP production, thereby reducing fatigue and increasing energy levels.

This benefit is attributed to the presence of phenolic compounds, including salidroside and rosin, which have been found to improve cognitive function, including attention, memory, and processing speed, thereby increasing productivity and overall performance.
This benefit is due to the presence of salidroside and rosin, which have been shown to regulate the body's circadian rhythms and promote relaxation, thereby improving sleep quality and duration.

This benefit is attributed to the presence of phenolic compounds, including salidroside and rosin, which have been found to have neuroprotective properties, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, thereby protecting against age-related cognitive decline and neurodegenerative diseases.

benefits_description: Rhodiola rosea reduces stress by decreasing cortisol levels and improving mood through increased production of neurotransmitters like serotonin and dopamine. Rhodiola rosea improves mood by enhancing the expression of genes involved in mood regulation, reducing symptoms of depression. Rhodiola rosea boosts energy by increasing ATP production and improving mitochondrial function, which results in reduced fatigue. Rhodiola rosea enhances cognitive function by increasing blood flow to the brain and improving memory performance. Rhodiola rosea decreases anxiety by reducing the activity of stress-related genes and decreasing symptoms of anxiety disorders such as generalized anxiety disorder and social anxiety disorder.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Rhodiola rosea reduces blood pressure by exhibiting antioxidant properties that counteract oxidative stress and lipid peroxidation in vascular walls. Rhodiola rosea improves circulation by enhancing vasodilation, a process facilitated by its ability to inhibit the activity of phosphodiesterase enzymes, which play a key role in vasoconstriction. Rhodiola rosea boosts oxygen delivery by increasing red blood cells, a result of its adaptogenic properties, which help to mitigate the negative effects of stress on hematopoiesis. Rhodiola rosea lowers inflammation levels in the circulatory system by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and eicosanoids. Rhodiola rosea enhances microcirculation, improves cardiac function, decreases triglycerides, and reduces platelet aggregation, all of which contribute to its overall beneficial effects on the circulatory system.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Rhodiola rosea reduces inflammation through its anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its protective effects on the digestive system. The plant prevents ulcers by inhibiting the growth of Helicobacter pylori bacteria, a common cause of stomach ulcers, due to its antimicrobial properties. Rhodiola rosea heals gastritis by reducing oxidative stress and promoting the production of protective mucus, which soothes the stomach lining. The plant stimulates digestion by enhancing the secretion of digestive enzymes, such as gastric amylase and pepsin, which aids in the breakdown of food. Rhodiola rosea also eases bloating by relaxing smooth muscle contractions in the intestines and protecting the gut from oxidative damage, while regulating pH levels and stimulating gut motility.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Rhodiola rosea supports thyroid function by modulating its response to stress, thereby regulating the production of thyroid hormones. The adaptogenic properties of Rhodiola rosea also regulate adrenal function, enabling the body to cope with stress without overproducing cortisol, which is responsible for reducing cortisol levels. Moreover, Rhodiola rosea improves insulin sensitivity, thereby enhancing hormone balance, and its antioxidant properties improve ovarian function. Additionally, Rhodiola rosea stimulates testosterone production and modulates estrogen levels, which can improve insulin sensitivity and maintain pituitary health. Furthermore, Rhodiola rosea boosts thyroid hormones and improves ovarian function, which are essential for regulating adrenal function and maintaining a healthy endocrine system.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Rhodiola rosea protects skin from UV damage and oxidative stress through its antioxidant properties, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation and promote healing. Rhodiola rosea heals wounds by stimulating collagen synthesis and angiogenesis, promoting tissue repair and regeneration. It fights fungus by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic microorganisms due to its antimicrobial properties, and soothes irritation by reducing pro-inflammatory cytokines and histamine release. Additionally, Rhodiola rosea regenerates tissue by increasing the production of growth factors and enhancing the integrity of the skin barrier, boosting immunity through its adaptogenic properties that increase the production of white blood cells. Furthermore, Rhodiola rosea prevents scarring by reducing the expression of fibrogenic genes, cures acne by inhibiting the growth of Propionibacterium acnes, and repairs damages by restoring the skin's natural barrier function.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Rhodiola rosea boosts lymph function by stimulating the lymphatic system's natural mechanisms, which in turn enhances lymph drainage and improves lymph flow. This herb reduces lymph swelling due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which also protect lymph organs from damage. The antioxidant and antimicrobial properties of Rhodiola rosea support lymph health by fighting off pathogens and promoting the activation of lymph cells. Additionally, Rhodiola rosea regulates the lymph system, reducing lymph inflammation and promoting a balanced lymphatic response. By activating lymph production and promoting a healthy lymphatic environment, Rhodiola rosea helps maintain a well-functioning lymphatic system.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Rhodiola rosea reduces muscle fatigue by exerting its adaptogenic properties, which enable the body to cope with physical stress, and also has antioxidant properties, neutralizing free radicals that cause oxidative stress. Rhodiola rosea enhances flexibility by improving muscle function, allowing for more efficient movement and reducing muscle spasm, a common cause of stiffness. Additionally, Rhodiola rosea reduces joint pain through its anti-inflammatory properties, decreasing inflammation and swelling in the joints. Rhodiola rosea also improves bone density by stimulating osteoblasts, the cells responsible for bone formation, which increases bone strength and reduces the risk of fractures. Rhodiola rosea reduces stress hormone levels, such as cortisol, and improves recovery rate by reducing muscle damage through its antioxidant properties and reducing inflammation, ultimately leading to faster and more efficient physical recovery.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Rhodiola rosea reduces stress by inhibiting the activity of cortisol, a hormone released in response to stress, thereby mitigating its negative effects on the nervous system. Rhodiola rosea improves alertness through its adaptogenic properties, which enhance the body's ability to cope with physical and mental stress. Rhodiola rosea enhances mood by increasing the production of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, which play a crucial role in regulating emotional state. Rhodiola rosea boosts energy by increasing the production of ATP, the primary energy currency of the cell, and enhancing mitochondrial function. Rhodiola rosea also regulates temperament, improves sleep, reduces anxiety, increases endurance, relaxes muscles, and supports memory by modulating the activity of various neurotransmitters and hormones involved in these processes, including acetylcholine, GABA, and melatonin.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Rhodiola rosea stimulates hormone production, particularly testosterone, by acting as an adaptogen, modulating the body's response to stress and promoting a balance of adrenal function. Rhodiola rosea supports ovulation by influencing the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis and modulating the release of gonadotropin-releasing hormone, which regulates the menstrual cycle. Rhodiola rosea enhances fertility in both men and women by improving the quality and motility of sperm and eggs, as well as increasing the thickness of the uterine lining, necessary for implantation. Rhodiola rosea improves libido by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters such as dopamine and serotonin, which regulate mood and sexual desire, and also by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the reproductive system. Rhodiola rosea also regulates menstrual cycles, reduces infertility, boosts sperm quality, protects the prostate from oxidative damage and inflammation, and supports pregnancy by creating a favorable environment for embryonic development and implantation.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Rhodiola rosea alleviates inflammation due to its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its therapeutic potential in respiratory health. This plant reduces respiratory stress by exhibiting antioxidant properties that mitigate oxidative stress and protect lung tissue from damage. Rhodiola rosea enhances oxygen absorption by increasing red blood cell count and improving cardiovascular function, thereby facilitating efficient oxygen delivery to the body's tissues. Additionally, it relieves congestion symptoms and improves lung function by reducing mucus production, which is attributed to its antimicrobial and mucolytic properties. Furthermore, Rhodiola rosea enhances immune response and improves breathing capacity, and reduces allergy symptoms and respiratory stress due to its adaptogenic properties.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Rhodiola rosea protects bladder from damage caused by its antimicrobial properties, preventing the growth of harmful bacteria. It reduces infections by inhibiting the production of toxins and neutralizing oxidative stress, while also exerting anti-inflammatory effects to alleviate irritation in the urinary tract. Rhodiola rosea fights kidney stones by its ability to regulate water and mineral balance, preventing the formation of stones. Additionally, it improves urine flow due to its diuretic properties, which increase blood circulation to the kidneys and improve their ability to filter waste. Rhodiola rosea heals kidney damage by promoting antioxidant activity, which repairs kidney cells and supports prostate health through its anti-inflammatory and adaptogenic properties.

properties_list.description: Rhodiola rosea has antioxidant properties due to the presence of phenolic compounds, which help protect cells from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals.

Rhodiola rosea is an adaptogenic herb that helps the body adapt to stress and promotes balance in the body's physiological response to stress, by modulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis.
Rhodiola rosea has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases.

Rhodiola rosea has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, exhibiting activity against certain microorganisms such as bacteria and fungi, although its effectiveness against a wide range of pathogens is not well established.
Rhodiola rosea has anxiolytic effects, reducing anxiety and stress in individuals, possibly by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine.
Rhodiola rosea may have antidepressant properties, improving mood and reducing symptoms of depression in individuals, although its effectiveness as a treatment for depression is not well established.
Rhodiola rosea is commonly used as an antistress agent, helping to alleviate symptoms associated with stress and burnout, such as fatigue and decreased motivation.
Rhodiola rosea may have neuroprotective effects, potentially helping to prevent or slow the progression of neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's.
Rhodiola rosea has been reported to have cytotoxic effects against certain cancer cells, although its effectiveness against a wide range of cancer cells is not well established.
Rhodiola rosea may have cardioprotective effects, potentially helping to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease by improving lipid profiles and reducing blood pressure.

parts_description: The most used parts of Rhodiola rosea for medicinal purposes are roots and flowers. The roots of Rhodiola rosea are rich in rosavin, roseroot extract, and salidroside, which are primarily responsible for its adaptogenic and antioxidant properties. These bioactive compounds contribute to the roots' primary medicinal properties, including anti-stress, anti-fatigue, and neuroprotective effects. The flowers of Rhodiola rosea, on the other hand, contain rosin and rosavin, which are mainly responsible for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties make the roots and flowers of Rhodiola rosea valuable in traditional medicine for their adaptogenic, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory effects.

parts_list.name: roots
parts_list.description: The roots of this plant contain major bioactive compounds such as rosavin, rosin, and salidroside, which exhibit adaptogenic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, providing primary health benefits including improved mental performance, reduced fatigue, and enhanced physical resilience.

parts_list.name: flowers
parts_list.description: The flowers of this plant are rich in major bioactive compounds like rhodiolosides and glycosides, which display adaptogenic, anti-anxiety, and anti-depressant properties, resulting in primary health benefits including reduced stress levels, improved mood, and enhanced sleep quality.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is used for the treatment of fatigue, stress, anxiety, depression, insomnia, and to improve physical and mental performance, particularly in athletes and individuals with high mental demands.
preparations.preparation_overview: Rhodiola rosea tincture is a widely used herbal preparation, commonly employed to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, stress, depression, fatigue, burnout, sleep disorders, ADHD, migraines, and memory loss. Rhodiola rosea tincture is a very common herbal remedy, frequently used in traditional medicine to treat a range of mental health issues. Its effects are often reported to be rapid and noticeable, improving mood and reducing stress levels. Rhodiola rosea tincture is typically made from the roots and leaves of the Rhodiola rosea plant, which contains bioactive compounds such as rosmarinic acid and salidroside. The process of making Rhodiola rosea tincture is moderately complex, requiring a combination of extraction and concentration methods to produce a standardized extract.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean fresh Rhodiola rosea roots and leaves, then chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped roots and leaves in a glass jar, leaving about an inch of space at the top.
Pour a neutral solvent, such as vodka or glycerin, over the plant material to cover it completely.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place, allowing the mixture to steep for 2-6 weeks, shaking it occasionally.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the liquid in a cool, dark place.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat stress, anxiety, fatigue, depression, and improve mental performance, memory, and mood, and to enhance physical performance and endurance.
preparations.preparation_overview: Rhodiola rosea capsule is a widely used herbal preparation for treating fatigue, stress, anxiety, depression, insomnia, mood disorders, burnout, brain fog, ADHD, and memory issues. It is one of the most common herbal supplements due to its effectiveness and popularity. The most reported effect of Rhodiola rosea capsule is its ability to reduce stress and anxiety levels, improve mood, and enhance mental performance. The preparation is made from the roots and leaves of the Rhodiola rosea plant, which is native to the Arctic regions. The process of making Rhodiola rosea capsule is relatively easy, as it involves drying and extracting the active compounds from the plant material, followed by encapsulation for convenient consumption.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean the Rhodiola rosea roots and leaves from a trusted source.
Dry the roots and leaves in a cool, dark place to prevent spoilage and preserve their active compounds.
Grind the dried roots and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered roots and leaves together in a bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powdered Rhodiola rosea blend and seal them tightly.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tea made from this plant is used for its adaptogenic and anxiolytic properties, helping to reduce fatigue, improve mental performance, and alleviate stress and anxiety symptoms.
preparations.preparation_overview: Rhodiola rosea tea is used to treat fatigue, depression, anxiety, stress, sleep disorder, hypertension, headache, migraine, ADHD and osteoporosis. This herbal preparation is commonly used due to its widespread availability and established efficacy. One common effect of Rhodiola rosea tea is its ability to reduce cortisol levels, improving mood and cognitive function. The tea is typically made from the roots and leaves of the Rhodiola rosea plant, which can be harvested, dried and then brewed in hot water. Preparation is straightforward, requiring only the addition of boiling water to the dried plant material, making it accessible to those seeking a natural remedy.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean the Rhodiola rosea roots and leaves.
Combine 1 part Rhodiola rosea roots and 1 part Rhodiola rosea leaves in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant container.
Add 1 cup of boiling water over the Rhodiola rosea mixture and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids.
Serve the Rhodiola rosea herbal tea hot or cold, depending on your preference.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Oil made from this plant is used to treat stress, anxiety, fatigue, and depression, and to improve mental performance, physical performance, and sleep quality.
preparations.preparation_overview: Rhodiola rosea oil is a herbal remedy used to treat stress, anxiety, fatigue, depression, burnout, insomnia, and cognitive function issues, as well as adrenal fatigue. This preparation is less commonly used compared to other herbal remedies. Its effects on the body are also uncommon, including improved mood and reduced symptoms of depression. Rhodiola rosea oil is typically made from the roots and flowers of the plant, and its production is challenging due to the specific conditions required for the oil's extraction and preservation.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean 100g of Rhodiola rosea roots and 50g of Rhodiola rosea flowers.
Dry the roots and flowers in a well-ventilated area, away from direct sunlight.
Combine the dried roots and flowers in a large glass jar and fill with a neutral carrier oil like grapeseed or sweet almond oil.
Seal the jar and place it in a cool, dark location, allowing the mixture to steep and infuse for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the herbal oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the herbal oil in a cool, dark location.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Salve made from this plant is used to relieve fatigue, improve mental performance, and reduce stress and anxiety by increasing mental alertness and endurance.
preparations.preparation_overview: Rhodiola rosea salve is a topical preparation made from the roots and leaves of Rhodiola rosea, a plant used in traditional medicine for over 500 years. It is uncommonly used to treat various health conditions, including stress, anxiety, fatigue, depression, insomnia, headache, muscle pain, memory loss and attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD). Rhodiola rosea salve has been reported to produce uncommon effects, such as improved mood and reduced fatigue, due to its high content of compounds like rosavin, salidroside and tyrosol. This preparation is made by steeping the roots and leaves in a carrier oil, a process that requires expertise and patience, as it is difficult to extract the active compounds without damaging them.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean 1 cup of fresh or dried Rhodiola rosea roots and 1/4 cup of fresh or dried leaves, and chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped Rhodiola roots and leaves in a saucepan with 2 cups of carrier oil, such as olive or coconut oil, and heat it over low heat for 2-3 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a small saucepan and heat it over low heat, stirring until the beeswax is melted and fully incorporated.
Combine the infused oil with the beeswax mixture and stir well to create a smooth, consistent salve, then pour it into small containers for storage.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Rhodiola rosea are experiencing insomnia, which can be exacerbated by high doses or prolonged use, developing anxiety in individuals prone to it due to the stimulatory effects of the plant, suffering from headaches caused by an imbalance in neurotransmitters, experiencing dizziness as a result of vasodilation and rapid changes in blood pressure, experiencing nausea when the plant is consumed in excessive amounts, reporting irritability in response to the plant's adaptogenic properties, suffering from stomach upset when Rhodiola rosea is ingested on an empty stomach, experiencing restlessness due to increased alertness and energy, experiencing tremors in individuals with a history of neurological disorders, and developing dependence on the plant with prolonged and frequent use.

precautions.precaution_name: Consult a healthcare professional before use
precautions.precaution_list_description: Interactions with medications such as blood thinners, diabetes medications, and certain antidepressants may occur.
Adhere to recommended dosages to minimize risk of adverse effects.

precautions.precaution_name: Avoid use in pregnancy and breastfeeding
precautions.precaution_list_description: Rhodiola rosea may cause uterine stimulation or lead to oxytocin release, affecting the fetus and neonate.

precautions.precaution_name: Monitor blood pressure and heart rate
precautions.precaution_list_description: Rhodiola rosea may lower blood pressure in individuals with hypertension.
Concomitant use with monoamine oxidase inhibitors or other serotonergic antidepressants may cause serotonin syndrome, a potentially life-threatening condition.

precautions.precaution_name: Avoid use with stimulants and caffeine
precautions.precaution_list_description: Combining Rhodiola rosea with stimulants or caffeine may increase the risk of adverse effects due to additive sympathetic nervous system stimulation.
Inform your healthcare provider about adverse reactions such as allergic dermatitis, headaches, or insomnia.

precautions.precaution_name: Keep out of reach of children
precautions.precaution_list_description: Children can be poisoned by ingesting Rhodiola rosea, which contains toxic compounds like rosavin and rosin.
Select supplements from reputable manufacturers that adhere to Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) standards.
Keep Rhodiola rosea supplements in a cool, dry place to prevent degradation of active compounds.

intro_study: According to a study published by "Biomedicine & pharmacotherapy = Biomedecine & pharmacotherapie", Rhodiola rosea has been found to have numerous positive effects. It has been reported to boost immunity, increase energy, and enhance mental capacity, making it a potential adaptogen to help the body resist stress. The study's findings highlight the immense therapeutic potential of Rhodiola rosea, suggesting its potential in the development of herbal nutraceuticals and pharmaceuticals for various health conditions.

According to a study published by "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", Rhodiola rosea has been shown to have several positive effects. It can enhance physical performance and work productivity, as well as alleviate symptoms of life-stress, fatigue, and burnout by improving cognitive functions and mental performance. Additionally, Rhodiola rosea has been found to have beneficial effects on cardiovascular and reproductive health by addressing non-specific stress damage.

According to a study published by Comprehensive Reviews in Food Science and Food Safety, Rhodiola rosea has been found to have several positive effects on the body, including decreasing depression, eliminating fatigue, and preventing high-altitude sickness. It also enhances work performance and increases mental alertness, making it a valuable adaptogen that can increase the body's nonspecific resistance to stress. Additionally, Rhodiola rosea has antioxidant and anticarcinogen properties due to the presence of several phenolic compounds, further solidifying its potential health benefits.

According to a study published by "Food chemistry", Rhodiola rosea extracts, particularly the 70% ethanolic extract, have shown high bioaccessibility of key compounds like salidroside, tyrosol, and rosavins after gastric digestion, with levels often reaching 100%. This suggests that Rhodiola rosea extracts may be effective in enhancing the absorption of beneficial compounds in the body. Furthermore, the 70% ethanolic extract has also demonstrated cytotoxic activity against human colorectal adenocarcinoma cells, indicating potential anti-cancer properties.

According to a study published by Phytomedicine: international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology, Rhodiola rosea extract SHR-5 has been shown to have a positive effect on physical fitness, significantly improving performance in neuro-motoric tests. Additionally, the study found that Rhodiola rosea extract SHR-5 also contributed to a reduction in mental fatigue and an overall improvement in general well-being. Furthermore, the results suggest that Rhodiola rosea extract SHR-5 may be a useful natural remedy for supporting individuals under stress during examination periods.
properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Rhodiola rosea are largely attributed to its antioxidant properties, which protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. This adaptogenic plant also exhibits anti-inflammatory effects, reducing inflammation and alleviating conditions such as arthritis, and antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi. Additionally, Rhodiola rosea possesses anxiolytic properties, helping to reduce anxiety and stress, as well as antidepressant effects, improving mood and reducing symptoms of depression. Its antistress properties help to mitigate the negative effects of stress on the body, while its neuroprotective properties safeguard against neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. Furthermore, Rhodiola rosea has been shown to exhibit cytotoxic effects against certain cancer cells and cardioprotective effects, which help to prevent cardiovascular disease by improving lipid profiles and reducing blood pressure.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Rhodiola rosea are primarily responsible for its adaptogenic and stress-protective effects, and include salidroside, a phenylethanol glycoside with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that has been shown to increase stress resistance and promote neuronal health by inhibiting apoptosis and enhancing neurotrophic activity. Rosavin, another key component, is a phenolic compound that exhibits adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory activity, and is thought to play a role in modulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis. Tyrosol, a phenolic compound, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, and has been shown to exhibit neuroprotective effects by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain. Caffeic acid, a polyphenolic compound, also exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity, and has been shown to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in various tissues. Quercetin, a flavonoid, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, and has been shown to exhibit anti-apoptotic and anti-inflammatory effects, and to modulate the immune response.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Rhodiola rosea for medicinal purposes are tinctures, which are often standardized to contain 3-6% rosavin and 1-2% salidroside, used to enhance mental performance and reduce fatigue. Capsules are another form of preparation, providing a controlled release of the active compounds and are used to alleviate symptoms of depression and anxiety. Rhodiola rosea tea is also consumed for its adaptogenic properties and to boost the immune system, and is often combined with other herbs for added benefits. Rhodiola rosea oil is used topically to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, making it a valuable addition to skincare products. A salve prepared from Rhodiola rosea is applied topically to relieve pain and reduce inflammation, often in combination with other herbal remedies.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Rhodiola rosea medicinally are to consult a healthcare professional before use to determine the optimal dosage and potential interactions with other medications. Follow dosage instructions carefully, as taking more than the recommended amount may increase the risk of side effects. Avoid use in pregnancy and breastfeeding, as the effects of Rhodiola rosea on the fetus or infant are unknown. Monitor blood pressure and heart rate regularly, especially if taking Rhodiola rosea for an extended period, as it may interact with certain medications or exacerbate underlying conditions. Be cautious with antidepressant medications, avoid use with stimulants and caffeine, report any side effects immediately, keep out of reach of children, use high-quality supplements only, and store supplements properly to minimize the risk of adverse reactions and ensure safe use.
intro_description: Rhodiola rosea, commonly known as golden root, is a medicinal herb primarily used to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression, as well as to improve mental performance and reduce fatigue associated with physical and mental stress. The herb is also used to address conditions such as insomnia, burnout, and post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD). Main health benefits of Rhodiola rosea include enhanced mood, improved cognitive function, and increased resilience to stress. The therapeutic properties of this herb are attributed to its adaptogenic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its neuroprotective and anti-fatigue effects. The bioactive compounds responsible for Rhodiola rosea's medicinal properties include rosavin, rosin, and salidroside, which are typically extracted and concentrated in herbal preparations such as tea, capsules, and tinctures.
